acceptedAnswer.text: SkillNest offers practical, project-based 6-week online cohorts covering Computer Basics, Touch Typing, block-coding (Scratch), Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint design, Internet Safety, and Generative AI prompt engineering.

mainEntity.name: What is the fee structure for the courses?

acceptedAnswer.text: We operate on a 'contact for pricing' model. Fee structures and available installment options are discussed transparently with parents during our free 1-on-1 trial and demo booking session.

mainEntity.name: What age group are these courses designed for?

acceptedAnswer.text: The primary curriculum is optimized for middle school students in Class 6-8 (Ages 10-14). We also offer foundational paths for Ages 8-10 (Class 3-5) and advanced skill roadmaps for Ages 14-16 (Class 9-10).

mainEntity.name: What hardware/software is required for the classes?

acceptedAnswer.text: Students need a working laptop or desktop (Windows or macOS) with a webcam and an internet connection. Mobile phones or tablets are not suitable as the classes require hands-on keyboard and mouse interactions.

mainEntity.name: Who teaches the classes at SkillNest?

acceptedAnswer.text: Classes are taught by founder Aanandita Uplopwar and senior trained educators who specialize in keeping young minds engaged using gamified, practical, and interactive instruction.
Yes, students receive a digital Certificate of Completion after finishing all project submissions at the end of their 6-week cohort.

mainEntity.name: What is the maximum batch size?

acceptedAnswer.text: To guarantee individual attention and debug code issues live, batch sizes are strictly capped at 5 to 10 students per online cohort.
